title: Tetragonanthus elatus
intro: Tetragonanthus elatus, also known as Tetragonolobus purpureus, is a perennial legume plant native to Asia, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as wound healing, pain relief, and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Tetragonanthus elatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tetragonanthus elatus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
